mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that reduces discount rates for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 2% in the CPIAUCSL series triggers central bank tightening expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.0%, or the signal's IC drops below -0.05 in rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in INDPRO (industrial production) and rise in UNRATE (unemployment) signals a supply-side shock combined with labor market weakness, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between INDPRO and UNRATE fails to explain SPX returns (p > 0.10) or 5d forward returns exceed -1.5% in 40%+ of interaction events.

mechanism_narrative: Rising HOUST (housing starts) combined with declining RSAFS (consumer spending) indicates overheating demand in real estate and underperformance in retail, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined HOUST-RSAFS signal shows IC < 0.02 over 3-year windows or 60d SPX returns exceed -3% in < 20% of interaction events.

mechanism_narrative: Strong PAYEMS (nonfarm payrolls) growth above 100k signals robust labor market conditions, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 50% of instances where PAYEMS exceeds 100k, or the signal's IC drops below 0.04.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in GDPC1 (GDP) growth below 2% signals weakening economic demand, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The GDPC1 signal fails to predict SPX returns (p > 0.10) or 5d forward returns exceed -2% in < 30% of triggering events.

mechanism_narrative: A rising FEDFUNDS rate combined with declining INDPRO signals monetary tightening and weakening industrial activity, causing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The FEDFUNDS-INDPRO interaction term shows IC < 0.03 or 20d SPX returns exceed -2% in < 25% of interaction events.

mechanism_narrative: A declining UNRATE (unemployment) below 5% indicates tight labor markets, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days through wage-push inflation dynamics.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 40% of instances where UNRATE falls below 5%, or the signal's IC drops below 0.05.
